frameworks.name: contour-based orientation estimation
frameworks.one_line: orientation extracted from object-bounding contours that delineate shape structure

frameworks.name: steerable pyramid (filter-based orientation energy)
frameworks.one_line: classical V1-style filter responses pooled as orientation energy across the image

frameworks.name: line drawing steerable pyramid
frameworks.one_line: filter-based orientation energy computed on contour-only line drawings

frameworks.name: image-computable encoding models of fMRI
frameworks.one_line: voxelwise prediction of natural-scene fMRI responses from competing orientation models

core_question: Does the human visual system encode scene orientation from object contours rather than from filter-based orientation energy?
key_assumptions: natural-scene orientation processing can be probed with image-computable models
contour-based and filter-based estimates can be made to diverge by patch selection
Natural Scenes Dataset fMRI supports voxelwise model comparison
manipulations: image patches chosen to maximize discrepancy between contour-based and filter-based orientation estimates
model class: Photo-Steerable Pyramid vs Line-drawing-Steerable Pyramid vs Contour-based
key_findings: behavioral orientation judgments matched contour-based estimates rather than filter-based energy
contour-prioritizing models better explained fMRI responses to natural scenes
contour models revealed discrete spatial patterns of orientation preferences in visual cortex
interpretations: the visual system encodes scene orientation primarily by extracting contours, the fundamental components of shape
filter-based orientation energy is insufficient to account for human orientation perception in real-world scenes
